title: Fully-Automated Publication Systems
summary: A standing Papyrus research track for end-to-end publication systems that automate research, editorial planning, drafting, publication, QA, review, and human override.

mission: Study publication systems as operational systems, not just model demos.
Prioritize durable workflows, editorial judgment, evidence handling, and failure containment over novelty alone.
policies: Track research automation, editorial planning, drafting, publishing, QA, review, and human override as one connected workflow.
Prefer primary materials, product documentation, system papers, source code, original evaluations, and operator testimony over derivative commentary.
Treat autonomy claims as unproven until the evidence shows what the system can do without human rescue.
Avoid promotional framing. Surface missing evidence, hidden human labor, brittle handoffs, and unverifiable benchmark claims.
Compare systems on provenance, repeatability, editorial control, and operational durability, not only speed or output fluency.

assignmentTemplates.title: System Architecture
assignmentTemplates.summary: Map the components, boundaries, orchestration layers, and external dependencies that make the publication system work.
comparisonQuestions: Which components are deterministic infrastructure versus model-driven agents?
Where are the hard boundaries between retrieval, planning, generation, validation, and publishing?
Which external systems or human operators are required for the workflow to complete?

assignmentTemplates.title: Agent Workflow
assignmentTemplates.summary: Explain how research, planning, drafting, and publication work moves through the system from trigger to output.
comparisonQuestions: What is the end-to-end handoff sequence from signal detection to published output?
Which steps are automated, supervised, or manually recovered?
Where does the system branch, retry, pause, or escalate to a human?

assignmentTemplates.title: Evidence and Provenance
assignmentTemplates.summary: Inspect how the system gathers evidence, preserves provenance, and prevents citation drift or fabricated support.
comparisonQuestions: What are the durable identifiers and provenance records for evidence?
Can an editor audit the evidence trail from draft claim back to source?
What prevents stale, weak, or fabricated evidence from entering the publication path?

assignmentTemplates.title: Evaluation and QA
assignmentTemplates.summary: Examine how the system measures quality, catches regressions, and decides whether output is safe to publish.
expectedEvidenceClasses: examples of blocked or corrected outputs
comparisonQuestions: What explicit quality gates exist before publication?
Which evaluations are automated and which still depend on human review?
How does the system measure regressions, false positives, and hidden failure modes?

assignmentTemplates.title: Editorial Control
assignmentTemplates.summary: Clarify how editors steer topic coverage, doctrine, approvals, and override authority without collapsing the automation loop.
comparisonQuestions: What can editors configure before a run versus after output is produced?
How are approvals, vetoes, and steering decisions represented in system state?
Can the system continue autonomously from prior editor decisions without silently drifting away from policy?

assignmentTemplates.title: Failure Modes
assignmentTemplates.summary: Document where the system breaks, stalls, hallucinates, repeats itself, or hides human intervention.
expectedEvidenceClasses: examples of incorrect or low-quality outputs
comparisonQuestions: What are the common failure modes and how visible are they to operators?
Which failures degrade gracefully versus silently corrupt output quality?
How much hidden human work is required to keep the system appearing autonomous?

evidenceRubric.title: Autonomy Scope
evidenceRubric.question: What parts of the publication workflow can the system complete without human rescue?

evidenceRubric.title: Editorial Control
evidenceRubric.question: How clearly can editors steer, approve, reject, or override the system?

evidenceRubric.title: Provenance and Auditability
evidenceRubric.question: Can the evidence chain be audited from published claim back to durable source records?

evidenceRubric.title: Evaluation Discipline
evidenceRubric.question: What explicit QA, scorecards, or evaluation practices block weak output before publication?

evidenceRubric.title: Operational Durability
evidenceRubric.question: How well does the system handle routine operations, retries, drift, and long-running publication cycles?

evidenceRubric.title: Failure Recovery
evidenceRubric.question: How visible are failures, and how much hidden human labor is required to recover from them?
